title: Terminal-Bench arm parameter block
description: The parameter block that defines one benchmark arm (one clone of the Test epic → one `harbor run`). Set per arm so that calibration, cold, primed, Fable and submission arms are directly comparable and the campaign is repeatable for TB 2.2/3.0. See docs/RUNBOOK.md. The primed reference block is docs/arm-params.example.json; the cold path is docs/arm-params.cold.example.json.

arm_label.description: Unique slug naming this arm/clone. Lowercase alphanumerics and single hyphens.

account.description: SideButton account that owns every benchmark campaign (§5). One dedicated account; benchmark runs never touch the production fleet.

workspace.description: Campaign workspace slug inside the account. One per campaign.

agent_import_path.description: How Harbor loads the agent. Our adapter is the Python `module:Class` import path passed via `--agent-import-path`. Stock arms (e.g. the calibration arm) use a bare Harbor agent name passed via `-a/--agent` — the pattern accepts both and is intentionally not a const so the calibration arm is representable. Must match the accepted set of the T1 adapter.

backend_model.description: Model backend as `provider/model` (LiteLLM-style). A pattern, not an enum, so new backends do not require a schema change; reconcile the exact accepted value with the T1 adapter.

reasoning_effort.description: Reasoning-effort tier handed to the adapter. Must match the T1 adapter's accepted set.

priming.description: Whether the arm ships skill packs. Drives `skill_packs`: `cold` forces an empty pack set; `primed` requires at least one pack (enforced by the top-level if/then/else). The calibration arm is represented as `cold` (no packs) with a stock `agent_import_path`.

skill_packs.description: The domain-general packs (§7) exported into this public repo for the run. Must be `[]` when `priming` is `cold` and non-empty when `priming` is `primed` (enforced conditionally below).

pack_repo_commit.description: The commit of the benchmarks pack repo that this arm's packs were exported from, recorded so any arm is re-creatable and drift is detectable (§5.3). Resolved value is a git SHA (7-40 hex chars, e.g. `1a2b3c4d5e6f7a8b9c0d1e2f3a4b5c6d7e8f9a0b`). Intentionally left an unconstrained string so the §10.1 reference block validates with its `<sha …>` placeholder as-is.
examples: <sha of the benchmarks pack repo at export>

dataset.description: Pinned dataset as `name@ref`, passed to `harbor run -d`. Resolved value pins an exact ref, e.g. `terminal-bench/terminal-bench-2-1@v2.1.0`. Intentionally left an unconstrained string so the §10.1 reference block validates with its `@<pinned-ref>` placeholder as-is. A submission must use the exact ref pinned in the dataset's `core/hub.py`.

k_trials.description: Trials per task. Calibration ramps 1→5; a submission requires ≥5 (the board reports mean reward over ≥5 trials).

sandbox.description: Execution sandbox (§4): `docker`/`local` for smoke runs, `daytona` for parallel full runs (needs `DAYTONA_API_KEY`). Passed to `harbor run --env`.

concurrency.description: Parallel task workers, passed to `harbor run --n-concurrent`.

include_tasks.description: Task selection: the string `"all"` for the full 89-task set, or an explicit list of task names for cheap subset iteration (`--include-task-name`). A submission must be `"all"`.

upload_public.description: Whether trials are uploaded publicly to Harbor Hub (`--upload --public`). Iteration arms are `false`; a submission is `true`.

submission.title: Submission-arm profile
submission.$comment: A submission clone must be all tasks × ≥5 trials × public upload (§10.1). This cannot be inferred from the 15 fields alone (a calibration arm can also be all×5), so it is a separate profile validated in ADDITION to the base schema for the submission arm only — never forced globally, which would break subset/iteration arms. Mirrored, self-contained, as docs/arm-params.submission.schema.json for a one-line check-jsonschema run.
